key_insight: AdsMind is a closed-loop multi-agent system that uses MLFF relaxation feedback to self-correct adsorption-site guesses and reliably find low-energy surface-adsorbate configurations.
engineering_relevance: It can greatly reduce the number of expensive evaluations needed for heterogeneous catalysis modeling while improving robustness over open-loop LLM-based search.
method: LLM agents propose candidate adsorption configurations for catalyst surfaces.
MLFF relaxation feedback is used to detect and correct poor initial guesses in a closed loop.
The framework is benchmarked across multiple LLM backends and validated with DFT on representative cases.
evidence_signal: Across AA20 and OCD-GMAE62, AdsMind achieved 100% and 98.8% success rates, with only 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case and improved DFT sign agreement on six AA20 examples.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the underlying MLFF relaxation and the tested benchmark scope, so transfer to very different surface chemistry may require additional validation.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
